content.text:
REVIEW-FINDING: 5.1.1(ii) WARN — both usage strings are inadequate: `NSCameraUsageDescription` is generic ("This app needs camera access." Info.plist:9) and `NSLocationWhenInUseUsageDescription` is placeholder text ("TODO" Info.plist:11)
Pierre: Apple rejects purpose strings that are empty, placeholder, or too vague to tell the user what feature needs the data. The camera string doesn't mention document scanning (the actual feature in ScannerView.swift:8), and the location string is literally "TODO" — plus there's no location code triggering that prompt at all. Rewrite both to name the specific visible feature, e.g. "SnapScan uses the camera to capture and scan your documents," and remove the location key if location isn't used.
